content: Curiosity-driven autonomous agents can use step-wise Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to reward intrinsic exploration at each decision step, rather than only rewarding final task success, creating agents that learn to prioritize high-uncertainty, knowledge-building trajectories over easily achievable low-value goals. This per-step intrinsic reward grading eliminates the sparse reward problem that plagues most current curiosity-driven reinforcement learning agents.
